In Andhra Pradesh, the state government administration was operating in absolute overdrive to prepare for a highly unique, deeply prestigious diplomatic event: the confirmed inclusion of Hyderabad on US President Bill Clinton's impending state visit itinerary. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On March 12, 1930, Mahatma Gandhi formally initiated the massive, globally significant Salt March, officially launching the massive Civil Disobedience Movement against the British Raj. Setting out from the Sabarmati Ashram with 78 highly dedicated followers, he aggressively marched 240 miles to the coastal village of Dandi to illegally produce salt from seawater. This brilliant, massive act of non-violent defiance heavily exposed the absolute cruelty of the British salt monopoly and fundamentally galvanized the massive, unstoppable Indian masses.

On March 12, 1947, the massive, highly influential Truman Doctrine was officially proclaimed by US President Harry S. Truman. In a massive address to Congress, he aggressively established the foundational geopolitical policy of the Cold War: the United States would actively provide massive military and economic assistance to any democratic nation threatened by authoritarian, communist expansionism. This massive doctrine fundamentally shifted American foreign policy away from isolationism and toward aggressive, global geopolitical intervention.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On March 12, 1993, the massive, highly tragic, and deeply horrific Bombay (Mumbai) serial bomb blasts occurred. In an incredibly aggressive, highly coordinated terrorist attack heavily masterminded by Dawood Ibrahim and the D-Company crime syndicate (with suspected ISI backing), 12 massive car bombs detonated across the city's commercial centers. The massive blasts resulted in the tragic deaths of 257 people and injured over 1,400, fundamentally altering the massive internal security architecture and counter-terrorism protocols of the Indian state.

On March 12, 1913, Yashwantrao Chavan, a massive, highly principled, and deeply austere Indian independence activist and politician, was born in Gujarat. He heavily served as the massive Chief Minister of Bombay State and later served with distinction in massive, senior cabinet roles in New Delhi. He aggressively reached the absolute pinnacle of Indian politics by becoming the 4th Prime Minister of India in 1977, heavily leading the massive Janata Party coalition that successfully dismantled the oppressive Emergency regime.
